fullstack.text: Fullstack · A REST endpoint will be used by both the web client and the mobile client. What is the most sustainable approach when sharing session management server-side?
options: Separate database schema for each client type
JWT or similar portable session token + common authorization layer
Cookie-based session only; emulating the same behavior on mobile
Storing session state only in client localStorage
Single user lock per server

fullstack.text: Fullstack · You want to improve the first paint time on a page coded with SSR; Data fetching is parallel but there is a risk of abuse for critical content. What do you do?
options: Loading all data on the client side with useEffect
Decoding critical data on the server and sending it via stream/preview, deferring secondary data
Sharing the CDN cache for all user sessions
Turn off Hydration and return to pure CSR
Avoiding inlining critical CSS

fullstack.text: Fullstack · N+1 query problem appeared in GraphQL schema. Which is the best permanent solution?
options: Keeping all resolvers synchronized
DataLoader-like bulk load/cache or join-based single query strategy
Hiding the problem with infinite scroll on the client side
Keeping the schema depth at a constant level of 1
Disabling HTTP caching

fullstack.text: Type safety (TS) and package versions shared in Fullstack · Monorepo conflict. The most balanced first step?
options: Dividing each application into a separate repo
Securing common packages with workspace protocol, single version policy and border modules (packlets)
Turn off type checking
Pushing all dependencies to the latest major version
Resolving sharing with copy-paste

fullstack.text: Fullstack · Webhooks reliability should be ensured in the payment workflow. The transaction is carried out without verifying the signature. What to add?
options: Hiding webhook URLs in robots.txt
Provider signature + idempotency + retry protection
IP only allowlist
Replacing webhook with synchronous HTTP call
Client side callback

game.text: Gameplay · You choose simulation synchronization for deterministic multiplayer. What is preferred in short delay?
options: Full server authority and lockstep on every frame — at appropriate bandwidth
All physics are free in the client; Last in a conflict wins
Just moving the visual effects to the server
Disabling UDP completely and only getting status via email
Testing network code in single-player mode only

game.text: Game · There are micro stutters in frame pacing and VSync interaction. GPU is waiting in the profile. First step?
options: Lowering resolution and adjusting GPU load with asset streaming / LOD
Turn off VSync and risk tearing
Randomizing physics step per frame
Making all shadows 4K
Locking update and render in a single thread

game.text: Game · Entity instantiation and frustum culling should be used together in a large open world. Trees are hanging. The problem is probably?
options: Separate draw call and material variant explosion for each tree
Too few polygons
Overuse of Instancing
No fixed time step
Sound engine setting only

game.text: Game · How to safely and atomically load DLC content from the main package?
options: Addressable entities / bundle manifest + signature verification + hot reload strategy
Embedding all DLC in build with Resources.Load
Requesting manual copying from the user
Only download ZIP without password
Using CDN without version number

game.text: Game · For detecting fraudulent accelerated progress in the game economy?
options: Relying only on client timestamp
Server-side odds limits, anomaly model and critical rewards server verification
All rewards are client randomly generate
Running anti-cheat only in single player mode
not keeping logs

mobile.text: Mobile · Background location permission should be requested “only when in use.” The most compatible pattern for iOS/Android?
options: Requesting all permissions repeatedly on first startup
Contextual explanation + progressive request; avoiding unnecessary background monitoring
Bypassing permission and constantly monitoring the device with UUID
Just writing in the manifest, not showing it to the user
Completely block app without location

mobile.text: Mobile · GC is triggered on list scroll under memory pressure. What is improved?
options: Recycler/List virtualization and image cache + object pool
Allocate new large bitmap at each item in the list
Pushing the entire list to the DOM at once
Combining strings in each frame instead of freezing animations
Turn off offline mode

mobile.text: Mobile · Deep link and secure redirection; Suspicious URL schemes should be filtered. Base defense?
options: Automatically accept all external schemas
Allowlist, verification and secure migration (app links / universal links)
Using http only
Disabling SSL pinning
Not logging intent

mobile.text: Mobile · To measure battery consumption and capture regression?
options: CPU profile only
OS battery reports + limit background jobs + sleep alerts and network/GPS usage logs
Asking the user to estimate the battery level
Not getting a profile in release mode
Keeping wake lock on all the time

mobile.text: Mobile · Offline cache sync; for conflicting regulations?
options: The last one to write always wins; no warning
Version vector / last write time + user resolution or automatic merge rules
Deleting the SQLite database and downloading it from scratch
Trusting only the server
Discard conflict silently

qa.text: QA · Best balance in testing pyramid for critical user flow?
options: Manual E2E only
Majority volume + contract/API + select E2E smoke
Load test only
Random click across entire environment
Running automation with production data

qa.text: QA · Flaky testing: network timeout. Permanent solution?
options: Set the timeout to 1 second and skip it
Deterministic stub, controlled standby and isolation + resource limit in parallel operation
@Ignore the test
Running with prod data
Full reboot every time

qa.text: QA · For prioritization in the pre-release risk matrix?
options: The last added feature is always the most critical
Focused scenario sets with impact × likelihood, frequency of use, and reversibility
Developer's favorite module
UI regression only
Maximizing the number of tests

qa.text: QA · Testing strategy with feature flags?
options: Opening flag combinations in uncontrolled production
Automatic + cascading canary for a small subset of the flag matrix
Always turn on all flags at the same time
Ignoring flag in test environment
close the logs
Screenshot comparison only
Rules-based browser controls + focus order and screen reader spotlight controls
Estimating color contrast
Not testing with keyboard at all
Implementing WCAG only on the marketing page

architect.text: Architecture · First decisions for an e-commerce that will scale for a decade. Which one is the most accurate?
options: 40 microservices from day one
Modular monolith + clear boundary + event-driven integration blueprint
All business rules in a single stored procedure
Continuous breaking change of the data model
Leaving Observability for last

architect.text: Architecture · Two teams overlap frequently on the same aggregate. In terms of DDD?
options: Dividing the Aggregate root and clarifying the bounded context and integration model
Locking a single database schema
ignore the conflict
Copy table for each team
Deleting all history without event sourcing obligation

architect.text: Architecture · SLA and error budget: multi-tenant SaaS. Priority?
options: Same noisy neighbor risk to all tenants
Quotas per tenant, bloat protection and individual scale units
Single server only
Autoscale without measurement
Single table to avoid sharing the database

architect.text: Architecture · To manage technical debt portfolio?
options: keep invisible
Measurable scenarios, service level impact and staggered payback (strangler)
rewrite all
Just watch the lint count
Not allocating time for the refactor

architect.text: Architecture · Data locality and GDPR focused design?
options: Storing all data in one region and ignoring the law
Regional storage, minimization, deletion/anonymization flows, and access control
Backing up without encryption
Releasing personal data in logs
Limiting deletion requests to manual email

DS-AI.text: Which measure is most important for education-production deviation as a whole?
options: Training accuracy only
Time window, distribution shift monitoring and retraining triggers
bigger screen
More hyperparameters
Increasing data without duplicating

DS-AI.text: To reduce selection bias in A/B testing?
options: Looking early with no stopping rule
Preliminary power analysis, fixed horizon and multiple test correction
Conversion rate only
single variant
Not doing random sampling

DS-AI.text: What does maintaining data quality for the feature store rely on most?
options: Batch ETL only
Schema agreement, validation, observability, and retrievable pipelines
CSV manual merge
Making the deduplication in the report
Not deleting raw data

DS-AI.text: Critical to model registration and reproducibility in MLOps?
options: Send the code by email
Experiment trace, artifact hash, dependency lock and evaluation metrics
sharing notebook
GPU brand
Manual deploy

DS-AI.text: There is a metric definition conflict in the BI dashboard. First step?
options: Separate board for each team
Single metric dictionary and data product ownership
hiding SQL
multiply data
shut down ETL

CS-NET.text: First step for suspected internal network discovery after corporate VPN?
options: Instantly reset all passwords
Isolating the scope of impact, preserving evidence, and initiating incident response
Antivirus update only
delete logs
turn off IDS

CS-NET.text: For risk of authentication weakness with TLS?
options: TLS only
MFA, device status and session availability checks
Password complexity is enough
JWT indefinitely
public S3

CS-NET.text: What is the risk of nonce repetition when using AES-GCM?
options: performance loss
Violation of password integrity and confidentiality; nonce uniqueness required
Nothing
Just longer key
padding oracle

CS-NET.text: Continuous process for dependency security in AppSec?
options: One scan per year
SBOM, automatic scanning and upgrade policy
npm trust
Not reviewing the code
Keeping debug open in Prod

CS-NET.text: Which is closest to the essence of the zero trust approach?
options: Trust in corporate network
Identity and context verification on every access; Network location alone is not enough
firewall only
Passwordless SSH
One factor everywhere

IS-MT.text: Which output adds the most value to stakeholder conflicts in the ERP process?
options: Directly coding a team's request
Common process model, RACI and approval points
No unnecessary development
Indefinite pilot
no test

IS-MT.text: For prioritization in ITIL incident management?
options: first come
Impact and urgency matrix
User voice only
random
closed call

IS-MT.text: When there is a change request in the project triangle (scope-time-cost)?
options: secretly add
Formal change control and impact analysis
Assuming the scope is fixed
skip the test
cut off communication

IS-MT.text: Balance of technology standardization and innovation in enterprise architecture?
options: Full freedom or full locking
Platform taxonomy and experiment areas
PowerPoint only
Each project has a different database
No documentation

IS-MT.text: CRM data quality is poor; Sales reports are conflicting. Permanent solution?
options: Correct the report manually
Master data, validation rules and ownership
Make all fields mandatory
Not merging duplicate records
Close the integration

CL-DN.text: Common basis for network isolation in multi-tenant cloud?
options: Single flat VPC
Account/VPC separation, security groups, and private endpoints
Public subnet everything
SSH is on
Passwordless API

CL-DN.text: Risk in production without Pod resource limit in Kubernetes?
options: None
Noisy neighbor, OOM and planning uncertainty
Log size only
Deploy faster
Better balancing

CL-DN.text: The main issue in choosing a blockchain in terms of the difference between permissioned chain and public chain?
options: Colour
Trust model and consensus cost/latency
Language
DNS
CDN

CL-DN.text: User protection for Web3 wallet signing request?
options: Unlimited approve
Permission scope, simulation and readable transaction summary
Pasting the seed into the application
Complete trust in every connection
Ignore phishing

CL-DN.text: For consistent configuration and updating across Edge deployment?
options: manually via USB
OAS/IaC, signed artifact and rollback
random ports
single server
no logs

SD_QUESTIONS.text: When the client connection is lost in a payment service, the user can resend the same payment request. The system sometimes collects twice from the same card, and sometimes the collection is made by the bank even though it appears as "payment failed". What should be the most critical design to solve this problem permanently?
options: Disabling the payment button for 10 seconds on the frontend
Managing the payment request with idempotency key, transaction status and external provider reconciliation
Telling the user not to refresh the page after payment
Assume automatic cancellation of all payments returning unsuccessfully
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Idempotency, distributed transaction, payment reconciliation.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: A team cancels an order in the REST API with the GET /orders/cancel?id=123 endpoint. Some orders were canceled when a search engine bot or link previewer visited these URLs. What is the most fundamental mistake in this design?
options: Short naming of Endpoint
Using the GET method for transaction with side effects and not providing secure verification of transaction intent
Sending the order ID with query string
API returns HTML response instead of JSON
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: HTTP semantics, secure API design, side-effect transaction control.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In a microservice architecture, the order creation process calls payment, stock, shipping and invoice services sequentially. When the stock service gives an error after the payment is successful, the system sometimes produces unfinished orders. Using a single global transaction is technically difficult and costly. Which is the more appropriate approach in this case?
options: Connecting all services to the same database
Designing saga-like compensatory transaction flow and consistent state management
Showing success message to the user when the stock service fails
Moving inter-service communication completely to the frontend
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Saga pattern, eventual consistency, compensatory process.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: A software team applies the same business rule separately in the frontend, backend and different microservices. Over time, the rules have been updated in some services and remain outdated in others. Users get different results for the same action. What is the most accurate architectural assessment here?
options: Since the business rule is used a lot, it should be copied wherever possible
Critical domain rules must be managed consistently, duplicate logic must be limited, and ownership must be clarified.
If it works correctly on the frontend, it doesn't matter if it behaves differently on the backend.
Consistency is never targeted in microservice architecture
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Domain logic ownership, consistency, maintainability.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: While an API endpoint returns the user profile, it also sends internal fields that the frontend does not use. These fields include account risk, customer segment, internal memo, and some personal information. Frontend does not display these fields on the screen. Why is this still problematic?
options: Data not visible on the screen is not considered to have been sent to the user at all.
Sending too much data causes both performance and data minimization and the risk of unauthorized information leakage.
As the number of JSON fields increases, security automatically increases
If the frontend does not use this data, there is no risk for the backend.
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Data minimization, API response design, privacy by design.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: A team runs manual SQL directly on the production database to resolve errors. Even if the problems are solved in the short term, it is not possible to track which changes were made when, by whom and for what reason. What is the most important deficiency in this process?
options: SQL should not be used
Production changes must be made through an approved, recorded, reversible and auditable change process
Database changes are only safe if done at night
Manual changes are quick, so no corporate processes are required
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Change control, auditability, production security.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: When cache was added to an application, performance improved significantly. However, when the price information is updated, the old price is shown to some users and a legal problem occurs. What is the real design problem in this case?
options: Using cache is always wrong
Cache invalidation, TTL and consistency requirement are not designed into business rule
It is unnecessary if there is a database cache.
The problem is always solved if users refresh the page
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Cache consistency, TTL, business rule impact.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In a system email must be unique. Backend first asks “does this email exist?” It queries and then adds a record. At the time of intensive registration, two accounts were created with the same email. Which approach solves this problem best?
options: Adding frontend control before backend control
Defining unique constraint in the database and managing the duplicate situation correctly at the application level
Telling the user to use a different email
Open registration only during business hours
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Constraint, concurrency, defensive data design.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In a QA process, tests only cover expected successful user flows. In Production, most of the problems occur in cases of double click, back button, disconnection, missing data, expired session and unauthorized access. What is the real weakness of this testing strategy?
options: Tests do not cover uncertainties in user behavior, error conditions and boundary conditions
Successful scenarios should not be tested
Manual testing becomes unnecessary if QA only writes automation
Less testing always results in faster delivery
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Negative testing, edge cases, risk-based testing.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: When a user changes his password in an application, old access tokens remain valid. Additionally, the refresh token duration is very long and there is no device-based session management. What is the most important security risk in this situation?
options: User logs in less often
Unauthorized access via compromised token or device may continue for a long time
Using tokens is always unsafe
Password change only concerns the frontend
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Token lifecycle, session invalidation, account security.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: A backend service sends a request to the external cargo API. When the shipping API slows down, service threads fill up and the entire ordering system slows down. The developer simply proposes to increase the number of retries. What is the risk of this recommendation?
options: Increasing the number of retries always relaxes the system
Uncontrolled retry can increase the load; timeout, backoff, circuit breaker and queuing should be considered together
External services never affect system performance
Using timeout hides all errors
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Resilience, circuit breaker, external dependency management.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In an application, uploading large files is done with a single request. If the user disconnects, the whole process starts over. Additionally, if the same file is uploaded twice, two different records are created. Which design is more professional?
options: Leaving it up to the user to reduce file size
Ensuring security again with partial loading, resume support, checksum and transaction ID
Keeping files permanently in frontend memory
If the upload fails, just show the error to the user
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Resumable upload, checksum, idempotent file processing.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: A mobile application has a large number of users using old API versions. The backend team wants to remove old endpoints. What is the biggest technical risk of this decision?
options: Backward compatibility may be compromised due to mobile applications not being updated at the same time for every user.
Reducing the number of API endpoints always improves system quality
Users using older versions can technically be ignored
API versioning is a matter of documentation only
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: API versioning, backward compatibility, release planning.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In a game development project, character movement is faster on some devices. The developer calculated the movement by increasing fixed pixels in each frame. What is the most accurate explanation of this problem?
options: Movement depends on frame rate; delta time or constant time step approach is required
The screen size of the devices must be the same
Physics calculations are not made in games
Speed ​​is synchronized if character animation is turned off
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Game loop, frame independent movement, simulation timing.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In an application, error messages are returned to the user in a very technical way. For example, the SQL table name, stack trace and server path appear. The developer says it “makes debugging easier.” What is the real risk of this approach?
options: User learns more technical information
Can leak information to the attacker for system architecture and vulnerability research
Error messages are not security related
Stack trace only affects frontend performance
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Information disclosure, secure error handling.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: An application appends the filename from the user directly to the server file path. The attacker tries to access different directories with expressions like ../../. Which security problem comes to the fore in this case?
options: Path traversal
DNS poisoning
Cache miss
memory fragmentation
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Input validation, file system security.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: A software team connected the test environment and production environment to the same database. Some real user data changed during testing. What does this error indicate is missing?
options: Environment isolation and secure test data management are missing
Test environment is unnecessary
Production data is the most accurate data for testing
Using database breaks tests
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Environment isolation, test data management.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In a team, everyone works on their own computer with different Node/Python/Java versions and different environment settings. Code that works for one developer does not work for another. What should be the permanent solution?
options: Replacing the computer of the developer who received the error
Documenting the development environment, using container or version manager and ensuring environment standardization
Encouraging each developer to use different dependency versions
Removing the test environment
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Environment reproducibility, dependency management.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In a monolith application, every small change requires redeploying the entire system. However, the team recommends switching to microservices only because it is “more modern.” Which is the most correct architectural approach?
options: Microservice is superior to monolith in every case
First, the current problem needs to be analyzed in terms of modularity, team structure, deployment independence and operation cost.
Monolith applications never scale
When switching to microservice, data consistency and operation problems are automatically resolved
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Architecture trade-off, monolith vs microservices.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: SQL queries are slow in an application. The developer wants to increase the server capacity immediately. However, most slow queries run without indexes in filter fields. What should be the first professional approach?
options: The query plan, index usage and data access pattern should be analyzed first.
Every performance issue is solved simply by upgrading the server
Database optimization is the sole responsibility of the DBA
Slow queries should not be shown to the user
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Query optimization, indexing, performance diagnosis.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: An application displays user input in HTML without escaping it. When the attacker adds a script to the comment field, it runs in other users' browsers. Why might backend validation alone not be enough in this case?
options: Encoding and sanitization appropriate to the output context is also required
XSS is just a database error
Frontend security never matters
Script execution is normal user behavior
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: XSS prevention, output encoding, input/output security.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: A team cannot find which commit caused which error after release. Commit messages are meaningless, there are no issue links, no release notes are kept. What is the fundamental engineering deficiency here?
options: Traceable development, meaningful commit, issue relationship and release management discipline are missing
If the number of commits is low, the quality is high
Release notes are for marketing purposes only
It is unnecessary to look back when a mistake occurs
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Traceability, release management, engineering process.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: Authorization control in a web application is done with middleware. However, some new endpoints remain outside this middleware chain. What should be specifically looked at in code review?
options: Whether endpoint names are short or not
Whether security controls are implemented by default and exceptions are explicitly managed
Alphabetical order of controller files
Whether the menus are hidden in the frontend
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Secure defaults, authorization coverage, code review.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: An application synchronizes long operations such as sending e-mail, generating reports and visual processing in the background within the HTTP request. Users receive timeouts. What is the more appropriate solution?
options: Taking long jobs to the queue/worker structure and showing the process status to the user
Increasing HTTP timeout to infinity
Telling the user to wait
Performing all operations in the frontend
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Async processing, job queue, user experience.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: A team opens the new feature directly to all users without using a feature flag. When an error occurs, one has to revert the entire application to the old version to get it back. What is the main benefit of the feature flag here?
options: Eliminates coding
Its features provide controlled opening, rapid closing and gradual start-up.
Removes database need
Completely eliminates the need for testing
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Release strategy, feature toggles, controlled rollout.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In an application, the user session is managed with a long-lived token stored in localStorage. Tokens can be seized when an XSS vulnerability occurs. Which issue should be considered together in this design?
options: Token storage method, XSS prevention, token duration and refresh mechanism
Token name should be longer
XSS is ineffective if localStorage is used
If the user logs out, all risk ends permanently.
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Session security, token storage, XSS impact.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: A backend service depends on multiple third-party APIs. When one of these providers slows down, the application keeps the user waiting without any explanation. Which is the better design?
options: Monitor dependency status, determine timeout, appropriate fallback and show meaningful status to the user
More calling all providers at once
Making the user wait indefinitely
Not logging third party errors
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Dependency resilience, fallback design, UX reliability.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: In a system, the audit log only keeps "transaction performed" information. However, it is not kept track of who converted which old value to which new value and from which IP. Why is this inadequate?
options: Event context remains incomplete for auditing, error analysis, and security review
Keeping details in audit logs is always prohibited
Only processing time is enough
Audit log is for performance measurement only
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Audit logging, accountability, forensic readiness.

SD_QUESTIONS.text: When a critical bug is fixed in a system, the developer commits directly to the main branch and deploys it to production. Testing and review are skipped. Even if the error is urgent, what should be the more balanced process?
options: A fast but traceable hotfix flow, minimum test, review and rollback plan should be implemented for emergencies.
No process required for immediate errors
Review is only done on large projects
Tests are always unnecessary with hotfixes
SD_QUESTIONS.measured: Hotfix process, release safety, operational discipline. 2. Data Science and Artificial Intelligence — 30 Questions

appQuestions.text:
Question 1 
Sudden performance drop

After the campaign starts in an e-commerce application, users begin to experience delays in the cart screen. No critical errors appear in the error logs. Database, API and cache systems run as separate services. What would be your first technical approach?
options: Increasing the server capacity and observing whether the latency decreases or not
Monitoring the request path end-to-end and distinguishing with metrics whether the latency is concentrated in the API, database, network, cache or external services
Simplifying the interface of the cart screen and reducing the processing load on the user side
Temporarily shutting down some reporting services during the campaign
Recreating database indexes and waiting for query plans to change

appQuestions.text:
Question 2 
Risk of bias in the artificial intelligence model

A credit pre-assessment model classifies a group of customers who are less represented in historical data as increasingly riskier. The overall accuracy rate of the model seems high. What is the most balanced evaluation approach?
options: Taking the model live because its general accuracy rate is high
Keeping the model's decision thresholds the same for all groups and observing the result
Dividing the data into subgroups and examining error distribution, data representation, decision thresholds and business rule effects together
Reducing the number of variables used by the model and switching to a simpler classifier
Trying to reduce variance by using a larger model

appQuestions.text:
Question 3 
Suspicious session activity

In a SaaS system, support requests are received from some users who say that they are not making transactions from their own accounts. In the logs, it is seen that the same user from different countries logs in at short intervals. What is the most appropriate first intervention?
options: Asking users to change their passwords and waiting for new requests
Blocking suspicious IP addresses and leaving the system running
Reducing the session duration and introducing new login rules
Invalidating suspicious sessions, assessing token and key risk, initiating incident investigations by preserving access records
Adding a security warning to the user interface

appQuestions.text:
Question 4 
ERP process incompatibility

While the sales team in a business wants the order approval process to be fast, the finance team does not want the same process to proceed without a credit limit check. The software side cannot start development because there is no clear requirement. Which is the healthiest analysis step?
options: Building on the flow of the sales team and leaving financial control to the next version
Making the finance team's rule mandatory and designing the sales screen accordingly
Modeling the current process, exceptions, data ownership, approval points, and performance expectations together
Using the standard order flow in the ready-made ERP package without changing it
Receiving separate requirements documents from all teams and combining common areas

appQuestions.text:
Question 5 
Unstable service on Kubernetes

A microservice is running on Kubernetes. Sometimes the service restarts, and sometimes it fails to respond even though it receives traffic. CPU usage goes up at times, but the same metric doesn't appear on every reboot. Which is the most accurate review approach?
options: Spread the load by increasing the number of pods
Reducing the deployment time by shrinking the container image
Correlating resource limits, liveness/readiness probe behavior, node pressure, and service dependencies
Move the service to a separate node group and observe the behavior
Putting the application log level in debug mode and monitoring reboots

appQuestions.text:
Question 6 
Technical question – idempotency in payment API

A payment API may receive the same payment request twice due to network timeout. Even if only one transaction is seen on the client side, two requests can be made to the server. Which is the most correct design?
options: Increase the request timeout value
Asking the user not to press the payment button a second time
Giving an idempotency key to each payment request, associating this key with the transaction record and returning the same result in the next request.
Storing the payment result in local storage on the client side
Manually checking the second payment of the same amount at the end of the day

appQuestions.text:
Question 7 
Inconsistent report in data pipeline

On a sales dashboard it looks like daily revenue increased twice in one day. In the raw data, some of the same orders were processed twice. Which is the best approach in terms of data engineering?
options: Hiding identical order numbers on the dashboard side
Adding data deduplication, transaction ID checking, data lineage tracking, and quality testing to the ETL process
Reducing volatility by converting the daily report into a weekly report
Moving order data to a larger storage area
Changing the summation function in the visualization tool

appQuestions.text:
Question 8 
SSRF risk

An application downloads a file from the URL it receives from the user. During security testing, it appears that this mechanism can be used to access metadata services on the internal network. Which is the most balanced defense?
options: Putting a longer character limit in the URL field
Queuing file downloads in the background
Making the user accept only HTTPS addresses
Checking the file type and limiting the allowed extensions
Implementing target address verification, allowlist, internal network/metadata access blocking, egress control and monitoring mechanism together

appQuestions.text:
Question 9 
Success of CRM feature

The “automatic customer prioritization” feature will be added to a CRM system. Management wants to see the business value of this feature. What is the most meaningful measure of success?
options: The feature will be released on the planned date
Number of times the screen is opened by users
Monitoring the sales team's feature usage rate, change in customer follow-up time, data quality and conversion rate together.
Increasing the complexity level of the algorithm
The feature looks the same on mobile and web interface

appQuestions.text:
Question 10 
CDN and old price problem

A product page opens quickly via CDN, but price changes are reflected late for some users. This situation leads to customer complaints. What is the best architectural approach?
options: Completely removing the use of CDN
Short-term caching of all data on the product page
Presenting price information from a separate data source out of cache or with a controlled invalidation mechanism
Putting a page refresh warning on the user's browser
To publish price changes in bulk at the end of the day

appQuestions.text:
Question 11 
Device dependent error in mobile application

While a mobile application works smoothly on new devices, it sometimes closes on old devices. The error does not occur for every user. What is the most appropriate solution?
options: Upgrading the minimum supported operating system version
Offering a lightweight interface option to older device users
Matching device model, OS version, memory status and screen flow with crash reports and making controlled corrections
Rewriting all screens of the application
Reducing animations and releasing a new version

appQuestions.text:
Question 12 
Technical question – data leakage

A machine learning model will predict at the time of ordering whether the order will be canceled or not. The "delivery completion time" variable was also used in the training data and the model showed very high success. What is the main problem in this case?
options: Training the model too quickly
Using an unknown variable at the time of prediction in training
Regression instead of classification
The data set is too large
The model has a high level of explainability

appQuestions.text:
Question 13 
Suspicion of lateral movement

It is seen that unusual connections starting from a single client in an enterprise network spread to different servers. The same client is logged in with an administrator account. What is the best first response approach?
options: Changing all network passwords at once
Isolating the client, preserving evidence, examining session and credential usage, removing the propagation path
Just start an antivirus scan
Restarting all servers
Temporarily slowing down network traffic

appQuestions.text:
Question 14 
Supply chain record with blockchain

A company wants to keep supply chain events on blockchain. However, he does not want trade secrets to remain open to everyone and cares that records cannot be changed later. What is the most appropriate approach?
options: Writing all data raw to the open chain
Keeping data in a central database and using blockchain only for marketing purposes
Keeping sensitive data out of the chain and writing hash/proof to the chain, designing permissioned access and key management
Using just a PDF signing process instead of blockchain
Establishing a separate chain for each supplier and performing the integration manually

appQuestions.text:
Question 15 
Proposal to completely change the architecture

A software team wants to switch the entire system to microservice architecture because the current system makes maintenance difficult. However, the product is actively used and delivery dates are close. Which is the healthiest decision approach?
options: Stopping the entire system and starting the redevelopment process
Leaving the microservice decision to management approval
Analyze maintenance cost, bottlenecks, team competence and risks and come up with a phased decomposition or modular improvement plan
Stopping new feature development altogether
Just adding documentation without changing the existing system

appQuestions.text:
Question 16 
Technical question â€” password storage

A system user wants to store their passwords securely. Which is the most correct technical approach?
options: Encrypt passwords with AES and keep the key within the application
Hashing passwords with SHA-256 and writing them to the database
Storing passwords with slow, salt-backed algorithms such as Argon2id, bcrypt or scrypt, additionally using controls such as rate limiting and MFA
Converting passwords to Base64 format to reduce readability
Marking the password field as a hidden column in the database

appQuestions.text:
Question 17 
Same metric, different result

Marketing and finance teams see the “monthly active customers” metric with different numbers in different reports. Both teams maintain that their report is accurate. Which is the most correct solution approach?
options: Accepting the report that appears to be more up-to-date as correct
Changing the reporting tool and switching to a single panel
Creating a common semantic layer that determines the metric definition, data source, time window, filters and ownership
Taking the average of two reports and using it in the management report
Updating customer data more frequently

appQuestions.text:
Question 18 
Production error due to environment difference

An application passes all tests in the test environment, but gives an error in production due to configuration differences. Which is the strongest healing?
options: Getting the testing team to write more manual scripts
Giving developers direct access to the production server
Standardizing environments with IaC and configuration management, establishing canary/rollback and observability mechanisms
Defining a quick hotfix process when there is a manufacturing error
Copying data in the test environment from production

appQuestions.text:
Question 19 
Lack of logs after the event

It is noticed that after a security incident, some of the logs are returned and old records are deleted. Unable to review. Which is the best design for the future?
options: Keeping the application log level in debug mode at all times
Storing logs as local files on each server for a longer period of time
Establish a centralized, time-synchronized, hard-to-change, access-controlled logging and retention policy
Writing user transactions to a separate table in the database
Making it a process to take screenshots when an event occurs

appQuestions.text:
Question 20 
Lost connection on edge devices

Edge devices on a production line send sensor data to the cloud. The network connection is interrupted from time to time. The system is required to continue without data loss. What is the most appropriate approach?
options: Reducing the frequency of shipping of devices
Restarting the device when the network is interrupted
Local buffering, sequence number, idempotent dispatch and use synchronization mechanism when it comes to connectivity
Generating sensor data only when connected
Moving the database in the cloud to a larger capacity

appQuestions.text:
Question 21 
Software architecture trend

A new module will be developed in a team. The requirements are not completely clear, but the module will talk to other systems in the future. Which behavior shows the tendency towards software architecture and abstraction more strongly?
options: First model interfaces, data contracts, dependency boundaries and points that may change
Quickly pull out a running screen and collect feedback
Start the project with the most popular framework
Gathering all the features to be developed in the same service
Embedding business rules into database procedures

appQuestions.text:
Question 22 
Technical question – ITIL incident, problem, change distinction

The payment system in an institution experiences an outage for two hours. The team first restores the service, then investigates the root cause and opens a planned release process for a permanent fix. How is this flow classified in terms of IT management?
options: First step is problem management, second step is change management, third step is incident management.
First step incident management, root cause analysis problem management, controlled release of permanent fix change management
All steps are within the scope of project management
The first step is change management, the remaining steps are event management
This process is only related to software testing management

appQuestions.text:
Question 23 
Recommendation system success

A recommendation system increases click-through rate, but users say they encounter less quality content. Management says "metrics are good, let's continue". Which is the most accurate data science approach?
options: Maintaining the model because the click-through rate is high
Retraining the same model with more data
Establish multiple goal metrics including satisfaction, content quality, long-term retention, and potential side effects
– ensuring impartiality by completely randomizing the suggestions
Training the model on only the most viewed content

appQuestions.text:
Question 24 
API rate limit exceeded

There is a rate limit in an API, but the attacker can exceed the limit with different token and IP combinations. Which is the strongest defensive approach?
options: Lowering the rate limit value for everyone
Only apply IP based limit
Establish distributed quota and abuse detection that evaluates user, token, IP, device trace and behavioral signals together
Remove limit information from API documentation
Returning a longer error message when limit is exceeded

appQuestions.text:
Question 25 
Consistency in a multi-region system

One system will operate in both European and Asian regions. The user profile must be opened quickly, and data consistency is critical in payment transactions. Which is the most correct architectural decision approach?
options: Using eventual consistency for all data
Carrying out all operations from a single center and closing other regions
Evaluating the consistency need for each data type separately and using strong consistency in critical operations and regional cache/eventual consistency in readable data
Running all databases independently in each region
Always redirect the user to the nearest location

appQuestions.text:
Question 26 
Technical question – Kubernetes rolling update and migration

In a Kubernetes application, the new version requires a change to the database schema. During the rolling update, old and new pods will work together for a short time. Which is the safest approach?
options: First close all old pods and then change the database
Allowing each pod to run Migration as the application starts
Going live with backwards compatible schema change, separate migration step, readiness control and rollback plan
Leaving the database change until after deployment
Always use blue-green instead of rolling update

appQuestions.text:
Question 27 
Admin panel prone to user error

In an administration panel, users accidentally close critical records. The system technically works, but there is a loss of business. What is the best solution approach?
options: Preparing longer training documents for users
Moving critical buttons to the bottom of the screen
Analyzing user flow and designing error-preventing interface, authorization control, rollback mechanism and transaction verification
Opening the panel to experienced personnel only
Reporting erroneous transactions at the end of the day

appQuestions.text:
Question 28 
SOC alarm fatigue

Hundreds of alarms occur every day in a security operations center. Analysts worry about missing critical events. What is the best improvement?
options: Raising all alarm thresholds
Hiring more analysts
Establishing a rule improvement process with alarm prioritization, correlation, triage playbooks and feedback according to the threat model
Turning off low-level alarms completely
Send separate e-mail notification for each alarm

appQuestions.text:
Question 29 
Adding personal data to the data warehouse

A new customer data source will be added to a data warehouse. The resource contains personal data, transaction history and support records. Which is the most correct initial design approach?
options: Getting all data in its raw form into the data warehouse
Creating a separate table that only analysts can see
Determining data classification, minimization, masking, access control, data lineage and retention policy together
Storing data only as CSV
Accepting all data as anonymous and making it available for reporting

appQuestions.text:
Question 30 
Monolith or microservice?

A newly established team will develop a platform whose product-market fit is not yet clear. The team is small, requirements change frequently, but high traffic is expected ahead. Which is the most logical architectural approach?
options: Setting up the entire system as a microservice from day one
Developing a single-file rapid prototype and leaving the architecture for later
Starting with a monolith whose modular boundaries are clear, observable and ready to be decomposed and then serviced when real scale and team needs arise.
Reducing application code by placing all business rules in the database
Setting up the entire cloud infrastructure to maximum scale before backend development
